风俗？正好最近我朋友在筹备献给法吉娜的仪式，我给你科普一下好了。
Customs? It just so happens that my friend has been preparing offerings to Phagousa recently. Allow me to explain them to you.
传说中法吉娜「把水变成佳酿，使信徒尽享欢宴」，但你知道吗，这不仅仅是个神迹，更是一种象征，其深层含义是分享与和谐。

Legend has it that Phagousa "turns water into fine wine so that their disciples may enjoy a great feast." However, did you know that this is not only a miracle, it is also symbolism for a deeper meaning? To enjoy sharing and harmony.
在奥赫玛，我们相信欢宴不仅仅是享乐，更是一种社交和交流的方式。通过共饮，人们放下戒备，敞开心扉。这种氛围下，往往能产生最富创造力的想法。

In Okhema, we believe that feasts and parties are not simply opportunities to enjoy a good time. We believe that they are also an important method of socializing and communication. It is through drinking together that people drop their guards and open their hearts. It is this kind of atmosphere that is the richest with creative ideas.
有趣的是，即便是普通的水，经过仪式后，人们也会觉得味如仙酿。这不正说明了信仰的力量吗？
The fascinating thing is that even normal water, once it has undergone this ritual, tastes like celestial wine to anyone who drinks it. Does that not show the true power of faith?
听闻人越多，佳酿的口味就越醇厚。所以…在奥赫玛，没有人会独自痛饮，我们总是习惯了一起面对困难，一起庆祝胜利。

I hear that the flavor of this fine wine grows richer the more people there are. So... no one drinks alone in Okhema. We prefer to face life's difficulties together, just as we celebrate our victories together.
Just what is this "blessing of Phagousa"?
什么？哦，那个啊...我们不是说好要保密吗？
What? Oh, that... Did we not agree to keep that a secret?
你也许会觉得那是普通的水，但我相信那是「加持了法吉娜祝福的奇妙之水」。只要我喝下去，就有站上演讲台的勇气。

You may think that was just normal water, but I believe that it is "miraculous water that has received the blessing of Phagousa." When I drink it, I gain the courage to stand upon the stage and speak.
听上去很荒谬，是吧？奥赫玛最著名的演说家，居然害怕面对观众。我每次站在台上，都感觉像是面对一群饥饿的野兽。我的手心冒汗，喉咙发干，脑子一片空白。

It sounds absurd, right? The most famous orator in Okhema is scared to speak in front of people. Every time I stand on that stage, I feel like I am facing a pack of hungry beasts. My palms start to sweat, my throat goes dry, and my brain goes blank.
但只要喝下那杯「法吉娜的祝福」，我就能镇定下来。我猜每个人都有自己的恐惧，想必你也不例外。你呢，你找到你那自己那杯「法吉娜的祝福」了吗？

But, if I drink that "blessing of Phagousa," I am able to calm myself down. I imagine everyone has things that they fear. I'm sure you are no different. What about you? Have you found your own "blessing of Phagousa"?
I don't have any more questions.
噢好的，我接下来还有几场演讲，恕我不能远送了。
Alright, I have a few speeches to give shortly. I shall have to leave you here, my apologies.
咳咳咳——各位，听我说，我们奥赫玛的繁荣离不开每一位居民的建设！每一位新生力量都至关重要，所以，我们得拿出来热情来欢迎我们的新朋友，加入奥赫玛！

Ahem — Everyone, hear my word! It is thanks to the hard work of its citizens that Okhema has come to be so prosperous! Every new strength that comes to be is crucial to our success. This is why we must show enthusiasm as we welcome our new friends to Okhema!
年轻人想收藏什么样的艺术品啊？给老头子说说，我给你点建议！
What kind of collectable items are kids into nowadays? Tell me, and I'll give you a few suggestions!
Why don't you introduce your collection first?
你要说这个我可就不困了啊！我家中藏品目前大大小小总共八百三十九件——不包括还没到货的订单和那些重复购买的——全都是和「全世之座，刻法勒」有关的艺术品。

Now that you bring this up, I'm not sleepy anymore! I have a total of <unbreak>839</unbreak> items in my collection — excluding orders that haven't arrived yet and those I've bought multiple times — all of which are art pieces related to the "Throne of Worlds, Kephale."
其中包括但不限于：雕像、印章、瓦罐、瓷盘、壁画、石板、刺绣、挂毯……
Including but not limited to: Statues, official seals, pottery, ceramic plates, frescoes, stone tablets, embroidery, tapestries...
可别小瞧了这些东西，它们每一样都代表着我对刻法勒虔诚的敬爱，是我信仰的寄托与证明！
It's nothing to sneeze at. Every single one of them represents my piety and love for Kephale — proof of where I place my faith!
多吗？我还觉得不够呢。那位泰坦是何其伟大！它是人类最初的缔造，也是最后的守护者——你瞧，现在我们之所以还能过上舒坦日子，不全仰赖它的庇护吗？

Do I have too many? I don't think I've got enough. Look how great that Titan is! It is mankind's first creation, and our final guardian — you see, isn't the reason that we're still able to live out such comfortable lives entirely due to its protection?
What experience would you like to pass on?
那首先必定是——选择靠谱的店！那些小作坊出来的东西看着物美价廉，实际上做工都糙得很，基本上逃不过「漆上有灰尘，釉上有指痕」定律。

The first thing is — choose a reliable store! Those smaller stores may seem to have good stuff for cheap, but the craftsmanship is actually terrible. They're basically doomed to have dust on the lacquer and fingerprints on the varnish, as we say in the industry.
你要是想买陶瓦工艺品的话，可以去西塔罗斯的铺子瞧瞧，他家的东西质量都有保障，就是最近要排很久的队。
If it's ceramic artworks you're after, you can check out Theodoros' store. His stuff is all fantastic, quality guaranteed — but it takes a while to get through the queue.
要是想买刺绣、挂毯之类的，可以去阿芙罗的裁缝铺转转，小姑娘手艺一顶一的好，很多时候成品做出来的效果都能超出你的预期。

If you want to buy embroidered works or tapestries, then you can rummage around Aphrea's tailor shop. The young lady there has exquisite skills, and more often than not, the finished craftsmanship will surpass your expectations.
至于石板…直接去克帖西芬的藏书所就行，不论正经的文献诗集还是不正经的小说绘画，在那儿基本都能买到，而且量大管饱。
As for stone tablets... just go straight to Ctesiphona's librarium. Doesn't matter if it's serious classics, or pulp picture novels — they've basically got everything, and in large quantity, too.
I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.
好嘞，跟你聊天还挺愉快的，有空记得多来和我唠唠嗑啊！
Cool, it's been real nice chatting with you. Come back whenever you like for the latest gossip!
你看看、看看——这么明显的瑕疵，竟然好意思说什么「货品既已售出、本店概不负责」？这合理吗、合理吗！

Look, look — this object is obviously flawed, and they dare claim "their store is not responsible for product quality once a sale is finalized?" Do you think that's reasonable!? Completely unreasonable!
嗯，确实还挺显眼的…不过问题不大，我可以帮你补一下。
Hm, it is rather obvious, isn't it... But it's not a big problem. I can patch that up for you.
我当然知道能补，但就是气啊！那些私人小作坊也就算了，这种有门脸的店铺怎么还能如此不讲究！
Of course I know it can be patched up. I'm just mad! I'd get it if they were just some small private workshop, but how can a legitimate store pull something like this?
要我说那些外邦人就是不地道，闲得没事非要搞什么「分期付款」！从预售到定金截止三个月，等个尾款小半年，就算结了尾款想收到货还得再两个月！

If you ask me, these outlanders just don't know how Okhema works! They sit around doing nothing and demand "payment in installments"! They give you three months after the presale to pay the deposit and by the time you make the final payment, six months have slipped by. Even then, it'll still be two months before you even get what you asked for!
等得久就算了卖得还贵，结果现在质量差还不管赔！以后就算他家的神像再怎么样式新颖造型独特，老头子我也绝对不买了！

If I have to wait, fine, I can deal with that. But they ask an exorbitant price, the quality is rubbish, and they won't refund me! I don't care how novel and interesting they make their divine statues... I'm not buying a single one!
下次还是找靠谱点的店铺吧，比如西塔罗斯小子家……
Why not try somewhere more reliable next time? What about that kid Theodoros' place...
别提他！说起他我就来气，原来多好一孩子，现在也和那些外邦人学坏了——
Don't even get me started on him! Just thinking about him makes me mad! He was a good kid, but he's picked up all those outlanders' bad habits...
那个黄金裔系列的陶罐你知道吧，我看着样式不错，想定制个同款的刻法勒陶罐，结果他跟我说至少要排队半年！你说气不气，啊，气不气！

You know about that Chrysos Heir dolia series, right? I liked the look of them and wanted to order a Kephale dolium for myself. Then he tells me I have to wait at least half a year for it! Don't you think that's ridiculous? Ridiculous, right!?
哈哈，你先别气，他那个系列确实是卖得太好，忙不过来了。
Haha, there's no need to get so worked up. That series sold really well. He just can't keep up with the orders.
哼…那小子手艺倒是没得挑，我之前买过他家那套十二泰坦的小雕像，那造型、那上色…别的不说，里头那个刻法勒雕像，到现在还是我最喜欢的。

Hmph... I have to admit, that kid does some good work. I bought that Twelve Titans statuette set a while back. The shape, the colors... Even if we ignore the others, the Kephale from that set is still my favorite to this day.
你说那套啊，我也很喜欢，可惜当时抢了好久都没抢到，最后只能高价收了几个散件。
You have that set!? I love that set. I was trying to get my hands on one for ages, but I never managed. All I could do was pay extortionate prices for a few spare pieces.
嘿嘿，我不仅买到了，还买了三套！你懂得——一套用来收藏、一套日常把玩、还有一套向人展示……
Hehe. I didn't just buy one, I bought three set! You know what it's like — one set for the collection, one for daily use, and one to show off...
唔…虽然没太听懂你说什么，但应该是在认可我对刻法勒深沉的热爱吧？哈哈，谢谢你！
Uhh... Though I don't fully understand what you mean by that, it sounds like you're expressing approval of my deep love for Kephale? Haha, thank you!
You sound like someone I've met before...
诶呦，看来你也认识和我一样的优质信徒啊？不错不错，有机会记得给老头子我介绍介绍！
Oh-ho! You know other outstanding disciples like myself? That's great! You'll have to introduce me sometime if you get the chance!
Don't you think that's a waste?
唉！怎么说话呢这孩子——信仰的事，怎么能说是浪费呢！
Ah! Watch your mouth, child — something done out of faith could never be described as a waste!
年轻人啊，我看你在旁边也听了好半天了，怎么，是也对泰坦相关的艺术品感兴趣吗？
I see you've been listening in on our conversation for a while, youngster. Well, are you interested in Titan-related artworks, too?
但我跟你说啊，这里头水其实可深了——新手容易买到高仿和次品不说，就算是正经货，想蹲个好价也不容易。
Let me warn you. There's a steep learning curve — beginners often end up buying imitation or faulty products. Even if they do get the real thing, getting it for a good price can be tough.
不过嘛，老头子看你有眼缘，倒是可以给你传授传授经验。来说说，你信仰的哪一位泰坦啊？
That being said, I can tell that you have fine taste. I can share some of my experience with you. Tell me, which Titan do you have faith in?
I have a little faith in all of them.
嚯…虽然别人的信仰我不好多嘴，但不够虔诚的话，可是没法的得到神明的青睐啊。
Haa... Though I don't like to criticize the faith of others, such a lack of devotion will never win you the favor of the gods.
呃…这名号没听过啊，是哪位泰坦的别称吗？
Hmm... I've never heard that name before. Is it a Titan's nickname?
I believe in the bat in my hands.
哈哈哈，现在的年轻人可真是…但毕竟我也不是什么老顽固，能理解、能理解。
Hahaha! Youngsters these days... Though, I'm no stick in the mud. It's understandable. Very understandable.
啊…有个雇主找我，你们先聊。对了，刚刚没来得及说，我最近新研究出一个涂装辅助机，虽然性能还不太稳定，但已经能运行起来了，回头你来给我测测。

Ah... One of my patrons is calling me. You two carry on. Oh, I forgot to mention — I developed a spray-painting machine recently. It isn't completely reliable just yet, but it's running. You should come and test it for me sometime.
哎——好好好！你先忙你的，我做完今天的祈祷就去找你！
Oh... Sure, sure! Do what you need to do, and I'll come and find you after my daily prayers!
呦，年轻人，需要老婆子为你占卜吗？
Oh, youngster: Would you like me to read your fortune?
诶呦喂，怎么一个两个总问这个。不如这样，异乡的年轻人，老婆子我先给你讲个故事吧：
Ahh, hey — why do people keep asking that? How about this, then, youngster from a faraway land: Why don't I tell you a little story...
在我的家乡——供奉着三重命运泰坦的雅努萨波利斯，千年前曾有位伟大的圣女。那位大人有幸聆听到{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的神谕，于是为了整个人类的福祉而盗取了{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}的圣火。

In my hometown of Janusopolis, where we worship the three Fate Titans, there was once a great and holy maiden a thousand years ago. This great lady had the grace of hearing {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}'s sacred prophecy, and with this stole the sacred flame from {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} to benefit humanity.
成为半神的圣女以「自我分裂」为代价，将获知的「创世神谕」传递给各个城邦。但…此举却反被有心人歪曲利用。

This maiden who became a demigod paid the price of self-fragmentation and passed on the Prophecy of Creation to all cities. However... it's a move that was twisted and misused by someone harboring ulterior motives.
你瞧——就连那位被神明垂青的圣女大人都无法真正掌控自己的命运，更遑论我们这些普通人呢……
See — even that maiden chosen by the gods wasn't even able to master her own destiny. What chance do mere mortals like us ever have...
当然啦，平日里占卜一下图个安心还是没问题的，不然我这种老婆子岂不是要饿肚子了，哈哈~
Of course, it's fine to tell a few fortunes now and then to set some minds at ease — otherwise an old woman like me would go starving, haha~
这个嘛，得看你究竟想要占卜什么——像我这种塔兰顿的信徒，向来都是讲究等价交换的。
Well, that all depends on what kind of prophecy you want. As a disciple of Talanton, I believe in equivalent exchange.
如果只是想问问最近运势如何，那么给我一个苹果就够了；但若是想知晓自己或他人的命运，那代价可就不是我能决定的喽~
If you just want to ask about your horoscope, then you can give me an apple. But if you want to know about your destiny or someone else's? Then that's a price I can't decide~
So, tell me about my horoscope.
嗯…挺好的。承蒙惠顾，一个苹果。
Mmm... It's pretty good. Thanks for coming — one apple, please.
That's it? Are you hustling me?
哪能啊，这么说吧——你在奥赫玛的这段时间运气非常不错，就算遇到些波折，最终也都会化险为夷。
No way, how about this — you've been very lucky during your time here in Okhema. Even if you encounter a few setbacks, you'll ultimately overcome them in the end.
Thank you for your auspicious words, but...
That was really phoning it in.
哦，你在意这个啊，放心放心——老婆子我给人占卜这么多年了，就算适当跳过些无关紧要的仪式流程，也完全不会影响结果的！

Oh, you really care about that, do you? Don't worry — this ol' granny has been telling fortunes so many years now that even if I skip over a few trivial ceremonial aspects, it doesn't affect the overall result!
咦…你身上没有苹果啊，那算了，这次就不收你费用了。承蒙惠顾，欢迎下次光临~
Oh... you don't have any apples on you. Then forget it, this time you can have it for free. Thank you for coming, and please come again~

I don't think this could be any more dodgy...
哈哈哈，你说得对，我也觉得这种事情挺不靠谱的。所以何必要纠结于缥缈不定的未来呢，好好享受当下的每一天还不够吗？

The old folks back home, they all reckon that spices have the power to fight off the black tide — and that there's a bug that's bigger than a whale lurking beneath the sands over there that could swallow a Titan, but will become docile and subservient in the face of certain spices...
…都是些乡间戏言罢了，在这圣城里只能当笑话来讲。而且，如您所见，凡事到了奥赫玛都会变得复杂起来——
...But that's all just rural superstition. Consider it all one big joke while we're here inside the holy city. Plus, as you can see, everything always gets a little more complicated in Okhema...
一方面，这里有些人会为香水和香油到底算瑟希斯还是法吉娜的赐福而争吵不休；另一方面，又有一些人认为自己没见过的香料必定不受泰坦的祝福，哪怕只是闻到都会有碍虔诚。

我过去也在云石市集摆过一个小摊，结果只一趟小解的功夫，摊子上的外地香水就被不知道什么人全都倒进了下水沟里……
I set up a stall in Marmoreal Market once, but when I came back from relieving myself, all my exotic perfumes had been dumped into the gutter by an unknown vandal...
虽然这件事发生以后，有不少本地的好心人帮我一起调查——可最后也没查出来什么结果，只能不了了之。

…这下您知道我为啥只能悄咪咪推销东西了吧？

Course I do. The thronging crowds are fantastic to watch from a distance like this... But once you draw closer or even join in, everything becomes utterly boring in an instant.
所有人的生活都是这样——醒来睁开眼睛，思考第一顿饭要吃什么；吃完了第一顿饭稍微做点自己的事，又开始为第二顿饭发愁…直到最后上床睡觉，然后开始新的循环。

Everyone's life is the same: Wake up, open your eyes, think about what to have for breakfast, eat that, catch up on personal affairs, then start worrying about what to have for your second meal... All the way up until bedtime, when the cycle starts again.
而如果因为种种原因，你连那一点「自己的事」都没得做…能确认自己还活着的方法，就只剩下了这样远远地看着别人。
And if for whatever reason you can't even get those personal affairs done... the only way to confirm that you're still alive is to stand at a distance like this, watching others.
距离产生美…不只是风景，人类也同样如此。
Distance is what creates beauty... Not just in nature, but also in people.
人们都说当{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}给你关上一扇门的时候，祂还会为你打开一扇窗……
They say when {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} closes one door, they always open a window...
…这话本身没错，但窗户只是用来看风景的。能从窗户翻出去的狠人，从来都是少数中的少数。
...That's true in and of itself, but windows are only for appreciating the view. The ruthless few who can climb out that window are a minority indeed.
人生或许真的是一个故事，但大部分人可能都不是故事的主角。
Life may really all be one big story, but most people aren't the protagonists of their own story.
如果你不想被打搅…告辞。
If you don't want to be disturbed... then so long.
唔，我还挺喜欢你这种干脆的人。拜拜。
Mmm, I quite like people like you who are straight up with me. Bye.
忙碌的人类，就像忙碌的蚂蚁一样缺乏意义。只不过……
Busy humans are just as meaningless as busy ants. And yet...
每当你觉得世界很糟糕的时候，它又看起来没那么糟糕。至少像这样远远看去，人间的烟火气还是能像画卷一样美丽。
Just when you think the world can't get any worse, it's not that bad. At least from a distance like this, the warmth of everyday life can still make up a beautiful picture just like this painting.
Let's talk about that warmth then.
哦。烟火气也是分很多种的，奥赫玛的烟火气又尤其与众不同。
Mmm. There's a variety of warmth to life, and Okhema's is particularly unique.
What do you want to talk about?
先想象一龛壁炉…旁边有一张餐桌。外面下着大雪，天气有点冷——一家人围着餐桌吃着热乎乎的饭菜。新鲜茴香的香气在蒸汽中盘旋。

Imagine a fireplace... next to it is a dining table. Snowdrifts are piled up outside, and it's a little cold... A family is gathered around the table eating a piping hot evening meal. The aroma of fresh fennel lingers in the steam.
…是不是感觉自己都有点饿了？但遗憾的是，咱真正回家的时候，要么就是谁因为什么不开心的事吊着臭脸，要么就是大家不知道都出去做什么了、空空荡荡一个人都没有。

...Aren't you starting to feel a little hungry? Unfortunately, when we get back to our homes, someone is unhappy about something or other, or everyone's off out of the house doing something else, leaving it empty.
这就是我说过的，远观和参与之间的区别啦。不过也没办法…就算是据说能为每个人都选择出最美好未来的{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}，那也有自己力所不及的时候。

That's what I meant — the distance between watching at a distance and joining in. But there's no other way... Even if {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} really can pick the perfect future out for everyone as they say, there will be times when the person themselves cannot manage to reach it.
喏——知道街那边有个裁缝店不？那位店长记得每个老主顾的喜好，也会按他们自己的生活境况为订单提出中肯的建议。
Hmm... You know that tailor down the street, yes? The manager remembers every regular's measurements and makes pertinent suggestions for their order based on their life situation.
…曾经，在我生活过的那个城邦，也有一位这样的店长。以前我还经常劝他，说我们那个穷乡僻壤订单太少，开裁缝店又那么累，不如退休算了。

...Once, back in the city where I once lived, there was also a shopkeeper just like this. I used to always urge him that he may as well retire, because there were too few orders in our remote little town, and running a tailoring business is hard work.
就像很多人一样——他一开始固执得很，觉得手艺就是自己生命的一部分，根本放不下自己的活计。怎么劝都劝不动。
Like many others, he was very stubborn to begin with — thinking that his craft was part of his life, his handiwork, of which he could never let go. There was no talking him around.
也就是到了前年，因为走路不小心摔了一跤，被迫在床上养了大半年…他这才发现，就算自己的店铺关门了，对街坊们也产生不了多少影响。

It was the year before last, he was careless and took a fall which put him in bed for half a year... Only then did he realize that closing up his shop didn't really create much impact on his neighbors.
所以，等他好了之后，我就劝他、也劝了很多街坊，跟着我们家搬到奥赫玛来——毕竟这里是世界上最安全的城邦，几乎受不到黑潮的威胁。只是……

And so once he got better, I convinced him and all the other neighbors to all pick up sticks and move with us to Okhema... After all, this is the safest city in the world, with almost no threat from the black tide. But it's just...
没什么。黑潮真正降临的时候…一切就是会发生得很突然。可能你准备什么都无济于事。一整个移民旅团，因为半道遭遇了袭击，到头来只有少数几家成功抵达了奥赫玛，拿到了难民身份。

It's nothing. When the black tide really comes... it'll all happen very quickly. Maybe nothing we could do would be of any use. Out of an entire caravan of migrants, only a few small families reached Okhema and obtained refugee status due to running into attacks along the way.
…很不幸，那位老爷子并不是幸运者之一。
...Unfortunately, that old timer was not one of the lucky ones.
Warmth of citizens who pay tribute to the Titans.
哦，这个也算是奥赫玛独特的景致了。
Oh, this is another unique worldview native to Okhema.
{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的云顶之城——它的选民总是充满信仰的热情。大部分吧。
{RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}'s cloudtop city — that god's chosen people are always filled with religious fervor. Most of them.
有时候真羡慕他们能如此投入自己的信仰…至少让生活不那么虚无，对吧？
Sometimes I really envy how they can throw themselves into their faith like that... At least, it must make life feel a little bit less empty, right?
只可惜信仰就像一种拼图——一旦被搅乱、打碎，人们就很难原模原样地再把它拼装回去。
It's just a shame that faith is like a jigsaw puzzle — once it's disturbed or smashed, it's very difficult for people to piece it all back together.
…虔诚的烟火气，它独特的美丽就来源于此。古代有些贤者会说什么「无知是福」——大概也就是这种意思吧？
...That's the essence of devout warmth and its unique charm. Some sages of old would say "Ignorance is bliss"... is that what they meant?
I don't have any more questions.
I won't disturb you from enjoying the view.
…坐在屋顶上的人看风景，而看风景的人在街巷中看你。
...People sitting on the rooftop look at the view, while people looking at the view see you in the streets.
没什么，我也不打扰你。拜拜。
It's nothing, I won't bother you. Bye-bye.
咦，是个生面孔。你好啊，我叫卢克雷提娅，是这里的兽医。
Oh, a fresh face? Hello there, my name's Lucretia and I'm the vet around here.
你是第一次来吗？特意来参观我们工坊的宝贝的吗——就是那些大地兽，它们可是我的大宝贝。毕竟…大地兽是吉奥里亚赐予我们的礼物嘛！
Is this your first time here? Did you come to see the precious babies in our workshop, our dromases? They're my big babies. I mean... Dromases are a gift from Georios!
是的，我刚来奥赫玛不久。
Yeah, I just came to Okhema not long ago.
原来如此，我就说你穿着打扮不像本地人。
I see. I was wondering why you aren't dressed up like a local.
没关系，你要是感兴趣的话可以在这儿多逛逛，「小家伙」们也喜欢热闹。
That's fine. If you want, you can just hang around here for a while. The little fellas like a bit of action.
不管怎么样，能认识新朋友总归是件好事。来，我给你介绍一下——旁边这些温驯的大家伙们可是我们工坊的至宝，别看它们块头大，平时可乖了。

In any case, it's always nice to make a new friend. Here, let me introduce you to these docile big 'uns next to you. They're real sweeties around the workshop and don't let their big size fool you, because they're normally very obedient.
好呀，我可以摸摸它们吗？
抱歉…最近恐怕不行，小家伙们最近表现有些异常，我怕出什么岔子。
I'm sorry... Not right now. The little fellas have been acting out a little recently, and I'm afraid it could cause trouble.
I'm sorry. I'm actually just passing through...
这样啊，抱歉，我还以为…唔，希望没打扰到你！
I see, sorry. I thought that... Well, I hope I didn't bug you there!
前些日子，我在给它们做检查的时候，表现得就很奇怪…放在以前，它们虽然害怕，但还是乖乖地排队，顶多就小声哼唧两句…不像现在……

A few days back when I was checking in on them, they were acting out a little... In the past, whenever they were afraid, they'd still line up obediently and would just grumble a few words... But now...
前些日子，我给它们做检查，小家伙们表现就很奇怪。放在以前，它们虽然害怕，但还是乖乖地排队，顶多就小声哼唧两句，不像现在这么狂躁。

A few days back when I was checking in on them, the little fellas were acting out a little... In the past, whenever they were afraid, they'd still line up obediently and would just grumble a few notes... But now they've turned feral...
为了你的安全起见，现在还是眼看手勿动吧。
Out of consideration for your safety, please look but don't touch.
What do you mean by "acting out"?
就是…突然出现了一些我从未见过的行为。要知道，我当兽医的年数虽然不算太长，但我家世代专门诊治大地兽，不客气地说，我见过的大地兽恐怕比我认识的朋友还多……

Well... They suddenly started behaving in a way they've never done before. You know, I may not have been a vet for very long, but my family has been looking after dromases for generations. To be blunt, I've probably got more of them as friends than I do people...
可前不久有个商队跟我反映的——他们商队的大地兽在回奥赫玛的路上，突然停下不走了，一直在「唱歌」，怎么哄都哄不动。
But not long ago, a caravan reported back to me that on the way back to Okhema, their dromases stopped and refused to walk. They started "singing" instead. They couldn't be pressed on no matter what.
那声音…据他们所说，非常尖锐，但你要知道它们平时的声音都是很低沉的，不会发出这么高音域的叫声。
That sound... according to the caravan, was really sharp. But you have to understand that normally their voices are very low, and they don't usually make high-pitched shrieks.
他们这个说辞我起初还不信，可昨天约莫是幕匿时分，我就听到了它们在这么叫，吓了我一跳。
At first, I didn't believe what they said. But yesterday around the Curtain-Fall Hour, I heard them doing it, and it freaked me out.
Have you been able to find out why?
还没呢，我正在记录它们的异常行为，希望能从中总结出某些规律。
Not yet. I've been recording their anomalous behaviors, and hope to be able to put together some conclusions then.
对吧！它们平时都很乖的，大家都夸它们听话，谁知道会引吭高歌突发扰民——附近的居民跟我们抱怨了好几次呢。

Right! They're usually so well-behaved and praised for their obedience. I never saw this coming, with the loud singing disturbing people — we've had to field complaints from neighbors several times now.
目前我有点怀疑，这是不是某种古老的习性——也许古老到在吉奥里亚将它们赐予我们之前，它们就生性如此，只是被驯养了这么多年，因为生活原因，很少对人展示出这一点。

Right now, I'm a little skeptical about whether it's some kind of ancient habit — perhaps a habit so ancient that it was how they naturally were prior to Georios gifting them to us. But, through years of domestication, they have rarely displayed this to humans, likely due to changes in their living conditions.
因为那声音…我听着感觉就像是远古的号角在呼唤着什么一样，我能感觉到它们有某种情绪包含在内，难道是…今年又要来一次「躁动的创生季」？

Because that noise... It sounds just like an ancient horn, rousing me to action. I can feel some special kind of emotion within it. Don't tell me... this year we'll be getting yet another "restless Creation Season"?
又到了动物交配的季节…
你怎么摆出这副表情？你是怕它们到处攻击人吗？你放心，它们只是古怪了点。
Why are you looking at me like that? Are you afraid they're going to lunge out and attack everyone? Don't worry — they're just a little kooky, that's all.
可能是到了青春期吧，但它们本性还是很温柔的，绝不会乱攻击人。工坊里有个新来的同事，最近总喜挠它们下巴，有时又会骑上去摸摸头，可它们也没有出现抵触情绪啊。

Perhaps it's because they've reached puberty, but they're still very gentle by nature and would never harm a fly. We've got a new coworker at the workshop who likes to tickle their chins and who sometimes rides them to pet their heads — but they never show the slightest resistance.
不过话说回来，我是真的觉得很奇怪…「小家伙」们到底怎么了？哎，可惜我目前还没有头绪……
But then again, I really do find it strange... What happened to the little guys? *sigh* Unfortunately I still don't have any clue...
奇怪…「小家伙」们到底怎么了？哎，是你啊！怎么，太喜欢大地兽又来看它们吗？还是说…想找我唠嗑？
That's strange... What happened to the little guys? Hey, it's you! What's up, can't get enough of our dromases? Or maybe... are you here to chat with me?
哈哈，「小家伙」们今天只是表现有些古怪，身体还是挺健康的，也没有受伤。所以我今天还算清闲，你想聊什么都行。
Haha, the little guys are just acting a little weird today. They're all healthy and injury-free. So I pretty much have a bunch of free time today if you want to chat about anything.
能给我介绍下大地兽吗？
Could you please tell me a little about dromases?
从哪里说起好呢？唔…就说这皮肤吧，你别看它们的皮肤表面有些粗糙，实际手感却像温热的鹅卵石，因为它们的体温比我们的要高一些，天冷的时候摸起来可舒服了。

Where to begin? Hmm... How about their skin: It may look rough on the surface, but it actually feels like warm cobbles due to their body temperature being slightly higher than ours. They're great to pet on a cold day.
不过我觉得最神奇的还是它们的胃，简直像是小型炼金工房，我都不知道它们是如何消化这么多奇形怪状的矿石的，光是咀嚼的声音我就听得牙疼。

But I think what's most amazing about them is their stomach, it's basically like a small alchemical workstation. I have no idea how they can digest so many weird shapes and minerals. Just the chewing sound makes my ears hurt.
最近还有什么异常表现吗？
最近啊，它们好像对某些地方特别敏感…比如说洗澡的沙坑，以前喜欢在里面打滚，现在说什么都不肯靠近。

Hmm. They seem to be more sensitive to a few places... Like for example the sandpit for bathing. They used to love rolling around in there before, but now they won't go near it no matter what you say.
还好我的同事们够聪明，提了几桶沙子过来泼在它们身上搓洗，它们倒是不排斥这个。
Luckily, my coworkers were smart enough to bring a few buckets of sand and pour them over themselves, cleansing their bodies with sand. They didn't hate it.
还有就是，最让我担心的一点——它们的眼神变了。大地兽的眼睛虽小，却特别有神。以前总是湿漉漉的很平静的，看着就让人感到安心。
And what's more, the thing that worries me most: Their eyes have changed. Dromas eyes are small, but very bright. They used to be moist and calm, giving you this feeling of contentment.
现在呢，它们偶尔会流露出一种…怎么说呢？既茫然又警惕，好像在提防什么，又好像对什么感到困惑一样的眼神…我和它们的饲养员试了好几次心理疗法，但都不见有效。

But now, they sometimes betray a hint of... How to put it? A look of simultaneous confusion and vigilance, as if guarding against something but also as if confused about something... I've tried therapy with their keeper several times, but it hasn't worked so far.
能给我讲讲大地泰坦的故事吗？
Can you please tell me the story of the Earth Titan?
哎呀，说起大地泰坦的故事，可有意思了。你听说过大地兽是怎么来的吗？据说啊，它们是从吉奥里亚的背上长出来的！就像是...嗯...就像是我们头上长头发一样自然。

Oh, the story of the Earth Titan is an interesting one. You ever heard about how the dromases came about? They say that they grew out of Georios' back! Just like... Um... Just like how the hair grows out of our heads.
还有人说，大地兽能预知灾厄呢。你别笑，真的！在我很小的时候有次我看到一群大地兽突然躁动不安，没过多久，旁边的巨树倒下了，吓得我那会儿腿都软了。

Some people also believe that dromases can predict disasters. Don't laugh, it's true! Once, when I was very young, I saw a herd of dromases suddenly grow restless. Not long after, a giant tree nearby fell. I remember being weak at the knees with fright.
不过，最有意思的还是一个老掉牙的传言。说是当大地泰坦开始沉睡的时候，大地兽就会闹腾。就好像在家长睡觉后偷偷恶作剧一样。

However, the most interesting one is also the ancient rumor: It claims that when the Earth Titan begins to slumber, the dromases will make a ruckus, like how children can prank the household after the parent has gone to sleep.
你说，会不会是现在吉奥里亚打盹去了，所以这些大家伙才这么不安生？
Would you say that Georios is now taking a nap, so these big fellas are starting to grow restless?
I don't have any more questions.
虽然大地兽们最近的表现是有些反常，但不管怎么说，大地兽是吉奥里亚赐予我们的礼物！
Despite the dromases behaving a little abnormally these days, they're still our gift from Georios no matter what happens!
你要是喜欢大地兽，希望你能多来看看它们，它们再怎么反常，表现出来还是很温柔粘人的…而且…说不定哪天它们就恢复正常了呢！
If you like dromases, I hope you can come visit them often. No matter how weird they might be, they're still always gentle, cuddly, and... maybe one day they'll make a return to normal!
I don't have anything to talk about.
没关系，等你对大地兽感兴趣的时候再来找我吧，毕竟我的生活就是这么枯燥，除了大地兽就是大地兽，也没什么别的可聊的了。

That's fine. Come find me if you're ever interested in dromases. After all, my life is really bland, and I've got a one-track mind when it comes to dromases. There's not much else for me to chat about.
虽然大地兽们最近的表现是有些反常，但不管怎么说，大地兽是吉奥里亚赐予我们的礼物！
Despite the dromases behaving a little abnormally these days, they're still our gift from Georios no matter what happens!
你要是喜欢大地兽，希望你能多来看看它们，它们再怎么反常，表现出来还是很温柔粘人的…而且…说不定哪天它们就恢复正常了呢！
If you like dromases, I hope you can come visit them often. No matter how weird they might be, they're still always gentle, cuddly, and... maybe one day they'll make a return to normal!
瑟希斯在上，看在研究的份上，就请让我进去吧……
For the love of Cerces... For the sake of my research, please let me in...
不行，这个真的不行，我也是听上面的吩咐，你也体谅一下吧。
No, this really is impossible. I'm just following orders here, please be understanding.
通融一下嘛，拜托...拜托了！哎呀，别走啊！
Please, can you bend the rules a little? Please... Please! *sigh* Come back here!
哎哟，吓我一跳！你从哪里冒出来的…而且…在这件事上无论怎么看都是浴场那边在为难我吧？
Yikes, you freaked me out! Where did you come from... And... no matter which way you look at this, it's the baths who are putting me out here, right?
别提了！浴场的老顽固们把我赶出来了，就因为我在里面装了几瓶水，真是小题大做！
Don't mention them! Those old pigheaded folks at the baths kicked me out just for refilling a few bottles of water inside. Good grief!
哦，对了，还没来得及自我介绍呢。我叫盖亚，是树庭的学者，目前正在研究水培植物的相关课题。
Oh yeah, I haven't had a chance to introduce myself yet. I'm Gaia and I study at the Grove. My research topic is currently looking at hydroponic plants.
一谈到研究有关的话题，盖亚整个人就兴奋了起来。她热切地望着你，你有某种预感，她似乎有许多话想跟你说。
Gaia grows excited as soon as she talks about her research. She earnestly looks at you, and you get the feeling that she's got lots more she wants to say.
可以跟我讲讲你的研究吗？
Could you please tell me about your research?
当然！你想听什么？我想想啊…就从我手头的研究说起吧？
Sure! What would you like to know? Let me see... How about starting with my current research topic?
打扰了，我对植物不感兴趣。
Sorry to bug you. I'm not interested in plants.
好吧，我理解，大家果然还是对大地兽之类的动物更感兴趣呢……
Alright, I understand. People are all way more interested in beasts like the dromases...
目前我正在进行一项课题研究，旨在探讨法吉娜祝福的泉水对植物生长与发育的具体影响。
I'm currently conducting a research project into the precise effects of fountain water that has been blessed by Phagousa on plant development and growth.
据说浴场的泉眼是法吉娜的恩赐，不同浴池的泉水各有不同的效果。除了治愈身心的常见功效外，传闻黄金裔专用的池水可能蕴含微量的泰坦之力。

Legend has it that the fonts of the spring water are a gift from Phagousa, and different baths can give you different effects. Besides the usual effects of healing both body and mind, rumor has it that waters in the Chrysos Heirs' bath contain vestigial traces of Titan power.
我推测，这种泉水或许能够逐渐激发黄金裔体内的潜能，在长期浸润下，因而得到了微量的泰坦之力。
I am guessing that this water may gradually trigger latent powers within the Chrysos Heirs, and with long-term submersion, they have gained slight amounts of Titan power.
因此，我正计划着系统采集不同浴池的泉水样本，并用它们灌溉我种植的「泪葱」，以观察不同泉水对作物生长的具体影响。
Therefore, I am devising a system to collect water samples from each of the baths, which are then used to water tear onions that I've planted to observe how different spring waters impact crop yield.
你这问题问得…瑟希斯在上，难道你没看到这其中的潜力吗？
You're asking that...? For the love of Cerces, can't you see the potential in this?
疑似含有微量泰坦神力的泉水，说不定能让植物开口说话、提升产量，又或者是发生了什么变异呢？
I am speculating that this spring water may contain traces of the Titans' power: Who knows if it might just make plants talk, or boost their yield, or cause some other kind of mutation?
想想看，一株会说「你好」的泪葱，多有意思啊！
Imagine how fascinating it would be to have a tear onions plant say "Hello" to you!
装洗澡水…听起来有点失礼。
Using bath water sounds... a little rude.
是有点，但一切为了研究嘛，就不要在意这种繁文缛节了。
Yes, kinda. But it's all in the name of science, so don't worry about any red tape.
而且，我相信黄金裔也是通情达理的，要是他们愿意听我讲解我的研究，说不定人家也愿意通融一二呢？
Plus, I believe that the Chrysos Heirs are reasonable people. If they're willing to hear me out about my research, perhaps they'd be willing to bend the rules a little for me.
What exactly are these tear onions?
泪葱就是泪葱啊，奥赫玛种了几千年的调味料…你家炒菜不放葱吗？
Tear onions are just tear onions. They're a herb that's been used in Okhema for thousands of years... You don't eat onions where you come from?
哎，你们这些年轻人是有所不知了，被黑潮所影响到的可不仅仅是我们和那些动物。
*sigh* You youngsters don't even know! It's not just us and the creatures that are impacted by the black tide.
植物们可不会说话，它们倾向于把自己的悲痛藏在根茎里，直到我们发现作物减产才窥得一二，届时奥赫玛恐怕也……
Plants can't talk, but they tend to hide their grief down inside their roots. We wouldn't notice if anything was going on until there was a drop in yields, by which time I'm afraid Okhema would...
算了算了，不说这些了，天塌了总归有黄金裔顶着呢。我还是继续想办法推进研究吧，现在在这儿一个劲地瞎操心也没用。

Forget about it, let's change the topic. If the sky fell down, there will always be Chrysos Heirs to prop it up. I had best keep pushing on with my research. There's no use worrying in vain about things now.
瑟希斯在上，看在研究的份上，能不能让我进去啊...哎呀，是你啊！怎么，找我有什么事吗？还是说，是哪位好心的泰坦指引你前来帮我的？
For the love of Cerces... For the sake of my research, please let me in... Oh-ho, it's you! What's up, are you looking for me? Or did a kind-hearted Titan guide you to come and help me?
Got time for a quick chat?
哈！当然有，你想聊什么？莫不是我的研究？太好了，终于有人对我的研究感兴趣了。来来来，我给你好好说道说道！
Ha! Of course, what do you want to chat about? Perhaps it's my research? That's great! Finally, someone is showing interest in my research. Come right this way, and let me tell you all~ about it!
能给我介绍下你的研究吗？
Could you please tell me about your research?
目前我正在进行一项课题研究，旨在探讨法吉娜祝福的泉水对植物生长与发育的具体影响。
I'm currently conducting a research project into the precise effects of fountain water that has been blessed by Phagousa on plant development and growth.
据说浴场的泉眼是法吉娜的恩赐，不同浴池的泉水各有不同的效果。除了治愈身心的常见功效外，传闻黄金裔专用的池水可能蕴含微量的泰坦之力。

Legend has it that the fonts of the spring water are a gift from Phagousa, and different baths can give you different effects. Besides the usual effects of healing both body and mind, rumor has it that waters in the Chrysos Heirs' bath contain vestigial traces of Titan power.
我推测，这种泉水或许能够逐渐激发黄金裔体内的潜能，在长期浸润下，因而得到了微量的泰坦之力。
I am guessing that this water may gradually trigger latent powers within the Chrysos Heirs, and with long-term submersion, they have gained slight amounts of Titan power.
因此，我正计划着系统采集不同浴池的泉水样本，并用它们灌溉我种植的泪葱，以观察不同泉水对作物生长的具体影响。
Therefore, I am devising a systematic collection of water samples from different baths, then use these samples to water my tear onions to observe how different spring waters impact plant growth.
浴场为什么不让你进去？
Why aren't you allowed in the baths?
嘿嘿，那些老顽固就知道阻拦我，我想可能是因为我先前在里面进行实验的事吧？
Teehee, those stubborn old goats are always trying to stop me. I think it's probably because of the experiment I used to run inside there.
有一次，他们在检修黄金裔的泉水管道，我在那漏水的地方种下了一株吉奥里亚根。到了第二天，那你猜怎么着？
One time when they were fixing a pipe to the Chrysos Heirs' baths, I planted a Georios root where the leak was. Guess what happened the next day?
它的根系长势惊人，还顺着管道那边一路生长，竟然把管道都挤爆了！
Its root system had grown at an exponential rate, along the sides of the pipe, until it burst it wide open!
可惜后来被浴场的人查出来，就把我赶了出来...但这些发现证明了我的理论是正确的！我敢打赌，要是能继续研究，说不定能培育出能抵抗黑潮的植物呢！

Unfortunately, the people at the baths found out and kicked me out... But these discoveries only prove that my theories are correct! I'm willing to bet that if I keep researching, I might even be able to cultivate plants that can resist the black tide!
你有什么有趣的发现吗？
像我们研究植物的，经常要去荒郊野外做调查，要不然就是窝在农田里观察作物。有趣倒算不上，只能说是一些疯狂往事吧。
Botanists like me are often researching in the wilderness or curled up in a field observing crops. I can't say much is interesting, but I will say I've got a few crazy stories for you.
我曾经把一些植物埋在靠近黑潮的土壤里，结果那些植物不仅没死，反而变得特别...阴郁。在靠近它们的时候，我总能听到一些发出奇怪的声音，好像在哭泣似的。吓死人了！

I once planted something in some soil near the black tide, but rather than dying, they instead became extremely... depressed. Whenever I got close to them, I could hear a strange noise that sounded like crying. It was spooky!
还有一次，我弄到了一些据说是尼卡多利神殿赐下的圣水。结果培育出了一些相当好战的藤蔓。它们会用根须主动攻击路过的小动物！

And this other time, I scored some holy water that was apparently from the Temple of Nikador. It ended up cultivating some pretty aggressive vines. They would actively attack small animals passing by with their roots!
我不得不把它们全烧了，好险没酿成大祸。
I had to incinerate the lot of them. Luckily it wasn't a complete disaster.
再见，我只是来打个招呼。
Goodbye. I just came by to check in.
哎呀，这就走啦？好吧好吧。对了，要是你能帮我弄到一些黄金裔的洗澡水，我会非常感激的！说不定下次见面，我就能给你看看会跳舞的泪葱了呢！

Oh, off so soon? Alright, alright. Oh, yes — if you could help me score some of that Chrysos Heir bath water, I'd be infinitely grateful! Maybe next time we meet, I'd be able to show you a dancing tear onion!
I've got to go. Talk to you later.
哎呀，这就走啦？好吧好吧。对了，要是你能帮我弄到一些黄金裔的洗澡水，我会非常感激的！说不定下次见面，我就能给你看看会跳舞的泪葱了呢！

Oh, off so soon? Alright, alright. Oh, yes — if you could help me score some of that Chrysos Heir bath water, I'd be infinitely grateful! Maybe next time we meet, I'd be able to show you a dancing tear onion!
Is there something I can help you with, dear customer?
您慢慢逛，有需要再叫我。
Take your time. Call me if you need anything.
客人。请问您需要什么吗？
Dear customer, do you need something?
我又不小心触发了对话。
I accidentally triggered this conversation again.
这里很空旷，很适合放松，对吧…您慢慢逛。
This place is quite spacious. It's perfect for relaxing, don't you think? ...Take your time browsing.
…看得出来，客人您一定闲得很。
...I can see that you have a lot of time on your hands.
既然如此，不知能否请您帮我一个小忙？外面有些喧闹，听声音像是几位不太受欢迎的客人在闹事……
If that is the case, I wonder if I could ask you for a favor? There is a lot of noise coming from outside. It sounds like there are some unwelcome visitors starting trouble out there...
身为侍者，我需要去调解一下，能请您暂时留在这里，帮我照看一下东西吗？
As an attendant, it is my duty to go and settle this. Could I ask you to stay here and keep an eye on things for me?
I'll go and mediate for you!
这…既然您坚持，也好吧。但容我嘱咐一句，对方要是不讲理，还请您别急着动手。毕竟看您的气势，恐怕对方非死即伤，我这边也会不太好办。

That... Well, if you insist. Although, a word of warning: If they are unreasonable, I ask that you do not rush to take physical action. I see you possess great strength and I fear that they could be injured or worse. That would make things difficult for me.
相对地，您可以报上我的名字。如果闹事的客人是如我所想的几位，只要说是「达罗」希望他们消停一下，应该会有用。
Conversely, feel free to use my name. If the unwelcome guests are the ones that I imagine, tell them that Daros would like them to calm down. That should help.
你半信半疑地答应下来，掏出球棒前去「调解」，场面一目了然，有几位看上去不太文明的家伙正在恐吓旁人。
Hesitant at his assurance, you pull out your bat and prepare to "mediate" the situation. When you arrive, it is clear that a number of uncivilized-looking guys are intimidating passersby.
这里毕竟未曾「开拓」，对方看到你的模样也毫不在意，甚至跃跃欲试地想要动粗。
As this place has not been "trailblazed" previously, the group is unfazed by your appearance. In fact, they seem to be raring for a fight.
You remember the words of the attendant...
Nonsense. I'll beat them up first and we can talk later.
动手前，你花了一刹那思考，说不定阿格莱雅之后会很啰嗦…要不，饶他们一命算了。
Before you make your move, you spend a few seconds in reflection. There's a chance that Aglaea might have something to say about this... Maybe you should let them go with their lives this time...?
你将侍者的名号报出，话没说完，对方的脸色骤变，立刻鞠躬道歉，一溜烟跑没影了——
As soon as you mention the attendant's name, the group's faces drop. Without saying a word, they bow in apology and run for the hills...
场面一度尴尬起来，围观的人都觉得莫名其妙，不一会就散了。
An awkward atmosphere fills the air as everyone watching the show struggles to understand what just happened. Before long, they are all gone, too.
Alright, I'll look after your things.
麻烦您了，客人。我去去就回。
Thank you, dear customer. I'll be right back.
他离开后，你竖起耳朵留意起外面的情况。
As soon as the attendant leaves, you prick up your ears and try to listen in on the situation outside.
嘈杂转为了寂静，随后转为了——悲鸣？你只能听见几句含糊不清的，听起来像是「悬锋的清洁工」之类的叫唤。
The rabble of noise turns to silence before turning into... wails? You only manage to overhear a few muddled words... Are they saying "Kremnos Cleaner"...?
最后再次回归平静，侍者像什么都没发生一样回来了。
After everything returns to quiet, the attendant returns as if nothing had happened.
消停了就好啊…谢谢您了，客人。
I'm glad that is settled... Thank you, dear customer.
啊啊，那些闹事的客人来自悬锋城，他们有人认得我…算是给了我这个老乡一个面子吧。
Ahh, those troublesome customers were from Castrum Kremnos. Some of them recognized me... Let's just say they showed their fellow countryman some respect.
不管怎么说，客人，我欠您一个人情。若是您再闲得发慌，我很乐意陪您消磨时间。
Either way, I owe you one, dear customer. If you are bored, I would be happy to help you kill some time.
轻松愉快些，无忧无虑的生者啊~时间实在太过短暂，领你一起去见塞纳托斯~
Relax and be joyous, you who live without concern~ Time is fleeting. Soon it shall take you to meet Thanatos~
…让您见笑了，客人。这是我以前工作时养成的习惯，还请多包涵。
...I am sorry you had to see that, dear customer. My old job left me with some bad habits. Please forgive me.
当然。您要是有想了解的事，请说，我尽量回答。
Of course. If there is anything you wish to know, I shall do my best to answer your questions.
师傅，你是做什么工作的？
What do you do for work, sir?
我是一位「侍者」，在保证浴场内整洁的同时，也为有需要的客人提供服务。
I am an "attendant." I ensure the cleanliness of the baths while providing services for customers in need.
…呵，客人，你的敏锐令人惊异，但也很容易招来危险……
...Hah. Dear customer, you are most perceptive... But this can get you into trouble, you know...
抱歉，我也言重了。不必担心，这里是奥赫玛，于刻法勒的庇护下，即使我全盘托出，我们的生活也不会发生任何改变。
My apologies. That sounded threatening. There is no need to worry. This is Okhema, after all. Under the protection of Kephale, even if I tell you everything, our lives will remain unchanged.
我的确是一位「侍者」，过去也同然。但在悬锋城那时，我的雇主更愿意称我为「清洁工」，或是「解决方案」。
I was an "attendant" in the past, too. However, back in Castrum Kremnos, my employers preferred to refer to me as a "cleaner" or a "problem-solver."
每隔一段时间，他们就会找到我，让我用一次「扫除」使塔兰顿的天平恢复整洁…至于过程，我不便多说，恐怕你也不会乐于听到。

Every so often, they would come to me and ask me to go on a "sweep" to restore the purity of Talanton's scales... As for how I did that, I will spare you the details. I fear that you will not enjoy them.
...Do you still do this work now?
不…悬锋城毁后，我辗转各地，却仅有{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的圣城愿意接纳一位蒙尘的人。那时我就下定决心，不到必要的时候，不再拿起清扫工具。

No... After Castrum Kremnos was destroyed, I roamed various places, but only the holy city of {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} would take in a stained fugitive like myself. I made a vow to myself at that moment that I would never again take up my cleaning equipment unless there was an absolute need.
元老院曾派人暗示我重操旧业，但楼上那几位尊贵的人替我出面「婉拒」了下来。时至今日，这种手段早已不再可行，也不再有意义了。

The Council of Elders once hinted that I should take up my old post. However, a number of esteemed persons came forward and "declined" on my behalf. Nowadays, such methods are no longer feasible; nor do they have any meaning.
当然。不管客人您是否真没听懂，慎重些总不会错。
Of course. Whether you understand or not, being cautious can never be a bad thing.
在您面前的只是一名侍者，浴场有需要服务的地方出现，我就会前往，仅此而已。
This man before you is merely an attendant. When my services are required in the baths, I shall appear. That is all.
侍者能讲的都是工作的故事，如果您不介意的话——
The only stories an attendant has are work stories. If you do not mind, then...
云石天宫每天人来人往，但总有些「独特」的客人会在我们职人的心里留下深刻印象，成为打烊后闲聊时的谈资。
Countless people go to Marmoreal Palace every day. However, there are always a number of "unique" customers that leave a deep impression on us attendants and become a topic of conversation later on.
例如「总穿着铠甲下池子的异邦战士」，「不去上层却喜欢呆在冷水池角落的黄金裔」，「自称塞纳托斯信徒的人」。大多面孔只出现过一次，也有些许成了常客。

For example, "the foreign warrior who always wore armor to soak in the baths," "the Chrysos Heir that always stayed in the corner of the cold pool rather than going upstairs," "the person that referred to themself as a disciple of Thanatos..." Most faces we only see once, though some also become regulars.
我印象最深的并非人类客人——一只年幼的大地兽，在公共池的边上落了脚，不顾旁人呼呼大睡起来，一泡便是四时三刻。

The customer that left the deepest impression on me wasn't even human... It was a young dromas that settled on the edge of the public pool and fell into a deep sleep, completely unfazed by the noises of others around them. Before it knew it, it had been soaking there for nearly four hours and three quints.
直到一位失魂落魄的饲养员把大地兽领走前，花园的蝴蝶都聚集并停驻在它的身上，令一众瑟希斯的信徒就地跪拜——那时，我才真正理解「神圣」一词的定义。

Before its dazed breeder came to collect the dromas, all of the butterflies from the garden had come to gather on its body, causing a group of disciples of Cerces to bow before it... It was at that moment that I finally understood the meaning of the word "sacred."
您可知道，除去污渍最好的工具是什么？每个人的见解不同，而我的答案是——「一切」。
Do you know what the best tool is for removing stains? People have different views on this, but my answer is... "Anything."
条拖与湿布，利器与火烧，有时还要借助瓶瓶罐罐里的洗剂，乃至是用另一种污渍去盖覆。不论何种操作，若是最终能达成洁净如新，那便是可行之道。

Mops or wet cloths, sharp tools or fire. Sometimes you need the help of a stain remover. Sometimes you even need another stain to cover the original. No matter what method you use, if you achieve cleanliness in the end, then you have chosen the correct method.
而那些墙上的难除顽渍，有时确实会让我们感叹无能为力，只好暂置。至于对策，等污渍蔓延开来，将墙面整个拆除重建，也不失为一种办法。

As for those stubborn stains that mark the walls, there are indeed times when we feel there is nothing we can do but leave them be for the time being. That being said, destroying the entire wall and rebuilding it after the stain has spread all over is also a potential solution.
欧洛尼斯的信者提示过我另一个思路，便是将污渍布置为艺术，令其与环境融为一体——但说实话，我至今也无法赞同。

Those who believe in Oronyx have suggested another way of thinking to me previously. That is, to see the stain as a form of art and allow it to blend into the surroundings... To be honest, I have never accepted that view.
曾有位异乡的客人，被法吉娜的秘酿击倒，在池中躺了几时。这个故事来源于他的梦话。
There was once a customer from another land who was struck down by Phagousa's ambrosia. He lay in the pool for hours. This was a story he shared when he spoke in his sleep.
「不，我无法接受你的心意，███。但我的确深爱着你。因为你与她的容颜一脉相承。」
"No, I cannot accept your love, ███. But I do love you, because you look like her."
「至今我仍然无法忘记她——金血般的眼眸与永夜色的长发。我看着她行于雅努斯的道路上，歌颂着欧洛尼斯的神秘，直至迎来塞纳托斯的拥抱，那便是我最珍贵的回忆——」

"I still cannot forget her to this day... her eyes like golden blood and her long, evernight hair. I watched her walk the path of Janus and sing of the mystery of Oronyx until the day she met Thanatos' embrace. My most precious memories..."
「所以，我会陪在你身边。因为你一定，也是她深爱的人。」
"So, I will stay by your side. Because you must be someone she deeply loves."
No worries. I'll leave you to it.
好的，请慢慢享受，客人。有需要再叫我。
Alright, please enjoy yourself, dear customer. If you need anything, please come and ask.
No worries. I'll leave you to it.
好的，请慢慢享受，客人。有需要再叫我。
Alright, please enjoy yourself, dear customer. If you need anything, please come and ask.
哈欠…巧了吗不是？饱饱地睡一觉，醒来就能看到这么有趣的陌生人。
*yawn* ...What a coincidence! After a long, fulfilling nap, I wake up to see a fascinating stranger like yourself.
你好呀！是从外邦来的客人吗？我代表奥赫玛大浴场欢迎你！
Hello! Are you a guest from another land? Allow me to welcome you on behalf of the baths of Okhema!
开个玩笑——我既不是泰坦也不是黄金裔，代表不了那许多人。清醒时人们自己做主，但生活乏善可陈；做梦时冒险精彩纷呈，可梦中人又无法自己掌控命运。

Just kidding... I am neither a Titan nor a Chrysos Heir. I cannot speak on behalf of that many people. When awake, people are masters of their own fate, but life is unremarkable. When dreaming, one can set out on a marvelous adventure, but will no longer be master of their own destiny.
既然你叫醒了我，那就来回答一个问题吧——就像冷水澡和热水澡，清醒和做梦你更喜欢哪个？
As you have woken me, let me ask you a question... If one is like a cold shower and the other a warm bath, do you prefer waking life or dreaming?
没什么能比掌握住自己的命运更加重要——{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}一定会赞赏你的。
Nothing is more important than being the master of your own fate... {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} would surely admire you.
为了把控住生命的方向，我们有时候需要舍弃许多利益…让人清醒的冷水澡，也会给不习惯的人带来冲击和痛苦。

There are times when we must abandon many gains in our lives in order to control its direction... A cold bath that brings sobering reality can cause shock and suffering to those who aren't accustomed.
痛并快乐着——祝你能在奥赫玛玩得开心！
You who feel the pain in happiness... May you enjoy your time in Okhema!
我们虽然无法控制剧本，但好的戏剧却总是能调动起人的情绪，让我们的内心像刚泡过温泉一样，鼓满生命的力量。
Though we have no control over the script, a good play can stir up the emotions of humans and stoke the power of life in our hearts — like we have been immersed in a warm spa.
一时无法掌控命运，未必不是一场好的冒险…人们固然无法阻止{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}带来的毁灭，但人们也总是能找到享受生命的办法。

Though our destiny may temporarily be out of our hands, it does not mean that an unpleasant adventure lies ahead... Though people cannot prevent the destruction brought about by {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}, they still find a way to enjoy their lives.
如果能跟随着一股洪流，做一个体验前所未有时代的冲浪者——那就简直是世界上最伟大的冒险了呀！
To be a surfer that rides the waves of a great torrent to experience an unknown era... That could be the greatest adventure this world has to offer!
真希望也有这样的命运能眷顾一个只知道躺着的懒人…祝你能在奥赫玛玩得开心！
I pray that such a fate could care for an idle person who spends all their days lying down... May you enjoy your time in Okhema!
哈哈哈哈，奥赫玛的哲学家实在有点多，要是人人都拦着你要辩论的话，那是挺累的！
Hahaha, Okhema is rife with philosophers. I'm sure it can become tiresome if everyone is stopping you to discuss such things!
不过没事儿，我也是一个只知道躺着的懒人——祝你能在奥赫玛玩得开心！
Fear not though, I am merely a lazy person who lives only to lie down... May you enjoy your time in Okhema!
好像曾经有位黄金裔说过，人生也不过是一场春秋大梦，就看谁先能清醒地意识到自己的命运。你相信吗？
I believe there was a Chrysos Heir who once said: "Life is but a grand dream. Let us see who is first to awaken to their true destiny." Do you believe that?
不相信也没关系，睡觉本身就很舒服——因为躺椅是世界上最伟大的发明！
It's fine if you don't. Sleeping itself is such a comfortable thing... Because the lounger is the world's greatest invention!
Tell me about the lounger, then.
没问题！你想知道什么？关于布料或者木材的知识，或者躺椅本身的历史？
No problem! What do you want to know? The fabric or wood that it uses? Or maybe the history of the lounger itself?
很久以前，躺椅曾是一种只有身份尊贵的人才能使用的家具。那些人会不惜成本地用上好的胡桃木、乌木、雪松木做成框架，冬天搭配细羊毛织成的软毯，夏天搭配清凉的白亚麻布。

A long time ago, the lounger was an item of furniture limited only to those of great importance. These people would spare no expense and use the finest walnut, ebony, and cedar wood to make the frames and pair them with a fine wool in winter, or cool white linen for summer.
咱大浴场里的躺椅当然没有这么复杂，加工材料就是普通的橄榄木还有棉布。不过橄榄木这东西可比那些松木结实多了——木材并不是越贵就越结实。

Naturally, the loungers at the baths are nowhere near as complex. Ours are made with simple olive wood and cotton. However, olive wood is far stronger than those pine woods... A more expensive wood does not necessarily mean a stronger wood.
说起来，在黑潮泛滥之前，还有些城邦会产一种叫做青冈木的木材…那家伙才是真的结实，不光能做家具，拿去当架桥的梁柱也一点问题都没有。

Come to think of it, before the black tide spread, there were cities that produced a wood called "blue oak"... That stuff was really strong. Not only could you make furniture with it, but it was completely reasonable to use it as a support in bridge construction.
唉，也不知道这个黑潮什么时候才是个头。希望不会到最后让我连个睡觉的地方都难找。
Ah, I wonder when we'll be done with this black tide nonsense... If it keeps raging like this, I fear I will be left without even a place to sleep.
…应该不会吧？反正不太像是咱有生之年里会发生的事！
...That wouldn't happen, would it? Not in my lifetime, at least!
Tell me about the history of the lounger.
有种说法是，最早的躺椅是给黄金世的祭司们专用的，一般人不能享受。
Some say that the earliest loungers were used by the priests of the Era Chrysea. They were not to be enjoyed by the commoners.
按照这个版本的故事，纷争世时期各城邦出现了元老院的制度，元老们为了从祭司手中夺取权力，将很多过去只有祭司才能使用的东西都推广到了民间——躺椅也在其中。

According to this version of the story, the Council of Elders began imposing restrictions across various cities during the Era Bellica in order to seize power from the priests. They took a number of items that were previously limited to priests and made them popular among regular citizens... Loungers were one of these items.
不过就我自己的看法…这大概只是卖躺椅的木匠为了让自己的产品卖得更好而想出的推销话术。
If you ask me, though... This is probably nothing more than a glorified sales pitch made up by carpenters to increase the sales on loungers.
这种事其实也不少见，像是什么谈恋爱就要送甜点、订婚就要送宝石戒指——这些我们耳熟能详的民俗最早也都来自商人的创意。

Such things are not rare. It's like having to give sweets and chocolates if you want to date someone, or having to give a ring if you want to get married... All of these familiar traditions were once nothing more than the creative ideas of merchants.
某种意义上，这也算人类文化自然发展的一部分吧。
In a way, this is also a natural part of human cultural development.
我看你也是个有故事的人……
I'm sure you have some stories yourself...
我？我没有什么故事啦，只是一个无所事事的奥赫玛本地人而已。
Me? I have no stories. I'm just an Okhema native with too much time on my hands.
真要说有什么特别的，大概就是我有家不回，天天住在浴场里这件事吧？
If there was something special... I guess it would be the fact that I never spend any time at home and always stay at the baths.
嗐——也不是什么复杂的故事，不过是有点讨厌自己的原生家庭，不是很想回去抛头露面罢了。
Ah... It's not a particularly complex story. I just don't like the home I come from and don't really want to show my face back there.
说起来，我还有个邻居，她可比我厉害多了…同样是有家不住，那家伙直接在云石市集找了一只不知道该叫桶还是筐的东西搬了进去！

Come to think of it, I have a neighbor that is even worse than I am... Not only does she never return home, she found a — I don't know whether to call it a bucket or a basket — in Marmoreal Market, and started living in it!
她有句名言，「睡眠是死亡的预演，死神未必有我们想象的那般邪恶」。仔细想想还挺有道理的——所以从那时起，我就决定人生的每一天，都要在最舒服的躺椅上睡到自然醒，嘿嘿。

She once famously said: "Sleep is a rehearsal for death. Perhaps Thanatos is not as evil as we imagine." If you think about it, it actually makes a lot of sense... As soon as I heard that, I decided that I would sleep on my beloved lounger every day until I've had my full of sleep, hehe.
I don't have any more questions.
You enjoy lying down. I'll be going.
好咧！躺累了泡澡，泡无聊了再躺回来享受美食——你试试就知道了，这才是美好的一天……
Alright! When I tire of lying down, I'll bathe. When I'm bored of bathing, I'll lie down again and enjoy some delicious food... Try it for yourself, you will realize that this is true perfection...
不过像你这样的人肯定闲不下来吧？劳碌命也真是辛苦…要记得忙里偷闲哦！
Although, I'm sure someone such as yourself does not get free time, right? A life of hard work is truly tough... Make sure you remember to enjoy a moment of leisure when you can!
行了，今天就先练到这吧。小毛病很多，但大问题只有一个——
Alright, that's enough practice for today. There are lots of small issues, but only one major problem...
你的出手实在太过仁慈。就比如刚才，我被路边的石头绊倒，你为什么不趁机刺我的后背？
You are too merciful with your attacks. When I tripped over a stone in the road just now, why did you not take your chance to stab me in the back?
在战场上，卑鄙并不可耻。敌人不会因你的仁慈而手下留情，你自然也不该如此。
There is no shame in being ruthless on the battlefield. The enemy will not go easy on you because of your kindness, nor should you.
对手的架势被破坏，你却不敢追击，反倒让自己露出更多破绽——那你不死谁死。
Your opponent loses their stance, yet you don't kick them while they are down, instead exposing even more weaknesses yourself... Then I guess it's your lot to die.
虽说死在战场上是一种荣耀，但送死和牺牲是两码事。倘若一点荣耀都没留下就死掉，除了家人，还有谁会为你哭泣？
Though it is an honor to die on the battlefield, self-sabotage and self-sacrifice are two very different things. If you die without leaving a shred of glory, who will cry for you but your family?
「犹豫就会败北，果断才不白给。」你走吧，回去之后把我教的技巧再多练习几遍。
"Hesitation leads to failure, decisiveness is never in vain." Be gone. Go home and practice the skills I taught you.
呵，现在的新兵大都只打过竞技场，根本不懂得战场是多么残酷。单挑和围殴可不能一概而论啊。

Hah, recruits these days... The majority of them have only ever battled in the coliseum. They know nothing of the cruelty of the battlefield. A one-on-one skirmish cannot be approached with the same logic as a military siege.
悬锋城人都这么不讲武德吗…
Do the people of Castrum Kremnos have no morals in war...?
悬锋城？不不，其实只有我而已，要不我怎么叫「狡猾的」尤利克赛斯呢~
Castrum Kremnos? No, no, this is just me. Why else do you think they call me "Ulixes the Cunning"?
别太为难人家小年轻了。
There's no need to be so hard on the young.
为难？等上了战场再思考这些就晚了。我从不在胸有成竹之前做出行动，所以大家都叫我「狡猾的」尤利克赛斯。
Hard on them? They need to think about things before they step out onto a real battlefield. I always have a plan in mind before I act. That is why they call me "Ulixes the Cunning."
Young people should lie! Cheat! And use sneak attacks!
这话有些偏激，但我确实提倡年轻人不要被规矩束缚。所以大家都叫我「狡猾的」尤利克赛斯。
That's a little extreme, but I do believe that youngsters should not allow themselves to be restricted by rules. That is why everyone calls me "Ulixes the Cunning."
在战场上，赢家才配说话。
Only the victor can speak on the battlefield.
是啊，博取胜利是战士的天职。不过比起蛮力，我更喜欢用技巧达成目的，所以大家都叫我「狡猾的」尤利克赛斯。
That's right. Claiming victory is the duty of a warrior, although I prefer using skill over brute force to achieve my goals. That is why everyone calls me "Ulixes the Cunning."
说到这里，老兵露出了匪夷所思的笑容，似乎为自己的这个称号感到骄傲。
With this, the veteran lets out a freakish smile, as if he feels pride for this nickname.
我看你的身材，像个战士。需要我传授些保命的技巧吗？
You have the body of a warrior. Would you like me to teach you some tricks to protect yourself?
这些都是战场上用鲜血换来的经验，务必认真对待。
All of my experience is earned at the cost of blood spilled on the battlefield. My words are to be taken very seriously.
Protect myself? I want to die in honor!
还真是典型的战士作风。不错，年轻人就该有些活力才好——但荣耀之死不等同于送死。
Classic words of a warrior. Not bad. A youngster like yourself should be spirited... But dying in honor does not simply mean letting yourself die.
I am scared of death. Please teach me!
哈哈，放心，有我在，你就无需从流血中总结经验了。
Haha. Fear not. With my help, you will never have to learn the lessons that come from spilled blood.
哈哈，看来你是个脑子活跃的年轻人。那好，等你有兴趣了，再回来找我吧。
Haha. I see you have an active mind, youngster. Very well, feel free to come back when you're interested.
我以前也是面对过黑潮的士兵。如今我虽不再踏上战场，却可以把经验传给年轻人，这也算一种荣耀。
I was once a soldier who stood against the black tide. Though I no longer walk the battlefield, being able to pass my experience on to the young is still a form of glory.
而我要教你的第一课，就是别被荣耀束缚……
And my first lesson for you is to not allow yourself to be bound by honor...
尤利克赛斯如豹子般迅速地弯腰，伸手向你的腿部扫去。就在你打算格挡前，他又把手收了回来。
With the speed of a pouncing leopard, Ulixes bends down and sweeps his hand towards your legs. Just when you are about to block it, he pulls his hand back.
I was just about to make my move.
You have no honor, old coot!
真打起来我未必是你的对手。但只要善加运用技巧，我这个老头也能从年轻人身上讨到便宜。
Though I may not be a match for you in an actual battle, I can still gain an advantage over you young'uns by utilizing my techniques.
这就是我的信条——宁失荣耀，勿失性命。在强于自己的对手面前，要利用一切优势、搏至无憾。
This is my creed... I would rather lose my honor than my life. When you're facing an enemy who is stronger than yourself, you have to use every advantage that you can and fight without regret.
…希望这能让新兵的存活率提高几分吧。
...I just hope that this will give our recruits a greater chance of survival...
技巧胜过蛮力，年轻人。要随时保持头脑清醒。
Technique is superior to brute strength, youngster. You must always keep your mind sharp.
I want to chat with you.
有什么能教给我的技巧？
What skills can you teach me?
问得好。很多悬锋战士踏上战场，就是为了迎接荣耀之死，所以他们都不怎么注重防护…这是习俗，没办法。

A fine question. Many Kremnos Warriors go to the battlefield in order to claim an honorable death. So they do not pay too much attention to defense... This is a custom, and there is nothing that can be done.
悬锋军视「在战场上战死」为最高荣耀，「凯旋」次之。「宁战死，毋荣归」是他们最喜欢的口号，而在床榻上了却一生则是最为人所不齿的结局。

The soldiers of Kremnos see "death in battle" as the greatest honor. "Triumphant return" is a close second. They like to say: "valorous death before glorious return," believing that peacefully passing away on your deathbed is the most contemptible ending.
理想的荣耀之死是与一位强大的敌手搏至无憾，但大多数战士没有那样的机遇。有些人会退而求其次，在战场上随便找个厉害的家伙打成一团，好让自己死得不丢份……

The ideal honorable death is for them to find a powerful foe on the battlefield and fight with no regrets, but the majority of warriors will not have this opportunity. Some of them settle for the next best thing and randomly find someone powerful on the battlefield to fight, so they can die with some semblance of honor...
如果你不是这种人，就老老实实带上盾牌，而不是把它放在家里供着。诗人们都说「带上盾牌意味着奔赴最险恶的战场」，但我认为每场战斗都应认真对待。

If you are not this kind of person, then you must carry your shield into battle instead of leaving it to gather dust at home. The poets say, "Carrying a shield means one is storming into the most perilous of battlefields," but I believe that every battle should be taken seriously.
短兵相接之前，战士们往往要花些时间靠近敌人。此时高举盾牌、护住脑袋，你就能挡下许多本该用脸去接的飞箭和激光。

Before engaging in melee combat, soldiers have to spend time closing the distance on their enemy. During this time, raise your shield high to protect your head. That way, you can block arrows and lasers that were supposed to be caught by your face.
另外，不要忽略了绑腿。这不起眼的布条能让你在行军时免去腿疼。倘若你在作战中受伤，它也能延缓你的死亡。
Also, do not neglect leg bindings. These unremarkable strips of cloth can save you from leg pain while marching, and even buy you more time if you are wounded in battle.
更多技巧就不一一列举了。你只需要记住一个要点：生存的关键在于细节。
I will not list all of my techniques one by one. You just need to remember one crucial point — the key to survival is in the details.
What is the black tide like?
说来惭愧，我只是跟黑潮打过照面，没有亲自同那些怪物作战。但那次经历让我难以忘怀……

Though I am ashamed to say it, I have only ever come across the black tide in passing. I never personally crossed swords with the monsters of the black tide. Though I will never forget that experience...
当时我率领小队出城执行剿灭任务，阴差阳错地进入了被黑潮掌控的区域——黑潮的污染极其可怕，只要踏入其中就会被影响。

The squad I led was dispatched from the city on an extermination mission when we unexpectedly entered an area under the control of the black tide... The black tide's contamination was terrifying... Just one step inside and it would take control.
我看见战士们被那黑色的泥潭吞噬，连尸体都没能留下，而后狰狞的怪物从黑水中浮现…等回过神来时，队伍里只剩下我了。

I saw soldiers devoured by that quagmire of the black tide. Not even their corpses remained. Then, fearsome monsters emerged from the black waters... When I came to, I was the only surviving member of my squad.
作为战士，我本该杀死那些怪物、为同胞们复仇…但我没有，我逃跑了。许多年轻战士因此骂我是懦夫，认为我应该和同伴们一块战死。

As a warrior, I should have killed those monsters and taken vengeance for my fellows... But I didn't. I ran away. Many young soldiers call me a coward for this. They believe that I should have died alongside my comrades.
想必尼卡多利也会唾弃我的怯懦吧。我失去了荣耀，却并不后悔——总得有人活着把经验传递下去。
I am certain that Nikador would despise me for my cowardice, too. I lost my honor, but I have no regrets... There has to be someone who lives to pass on their experience.
我要怎样成为一名战士？
How can I become a warrior?
悬锋城战士虽多，却也不是每个人都能成为战士。在踏上这条路之前，你得先做好觉悟。
There are many warriors in Castrum Kremnos, but not everyone can become one. One must be properly prepared before embarking on this path.
通过考核的新兵会首先接受体能、武艺和阵型的训练，从门关月一直持续到收获月。在随后的纷争月中，那些表现优异者会被编入军队出战。

Recruits that pass examinations will receive physical training, martial arts training, and formation training from the Month of Gate until the Month of Reaping. In the following Month of Strife, those who performed outstandingly will be entered into the army to fight.
只要能活下来，战火的淬炼就会把你变成独当一面的老兵。倘若积累了足够的荣耀，你还能得到一面属于自己的盾牌。
If you are able to survive, the flames of war will temper you into an elite veteran. If you accumulate enough glory, you will even be granted your own shield.
不过现在，大多数年轻人的盾牌都来自父母——出征之前，战士们会举行名为授盾礼的告别仪式，希望后代能将盾牌和荣誉一并传承下去。

However, the majority of shields youngsters use nowadays come from their parents... Before they go out on expeditions, warriors take part in a farewell ceremony known as the Rite of the Shield. The hope is that the descendants will continue to pass down honor alongside their shield as a legacy.
「要么高举着它凯旋，要么躺在上面荣归。」很有气势的俗语。但那些战败后活着回来的战士同样值得尊敬。
"Return holding it high in triumph, or return with honor laid atop it." It is a powerful old saying. However, even those warriors who return alive after defeat deserve to be respected, too.
倘若每场败仗都是全军覆没，我们永远也不会知道失败的原因。没有永恒的胜利，唯经验长存。
If every failed battle left no survivors, we would never know what it was that led to our defeat. There is no such thing as an eternal victory. Only experience lasts forever.
Very well. We don't want you biting off more than you can chew.
Have you got anything good for me?
善加利用这些东西，你就能从战场上活着回来。
Use these well, and you can return from the battlefield with your life.
Let's leave it there for now.
Remember to always think before you take action.
到时候就这么说吧：「我是赫克鲁斯。我以荣耀发誓，宁死也不抛弃同伴！」
I know. I'll say: "I am Herklas. I vow upon my honor that I won't abandon my friends, even in death!"
…不，感觉还是缺乏气势，得想个更帅气的出场词才行。身为克拉特鲁斯大人亲手提拔的悬锋伙友，可不能丢份啊。
...No, still lacks some panache. Got to have a more dashing opener. As a stalwart Kremnos vanguard handpicked by Lord Krateros, I mustn't lose face.
对，终有一天，我会在战场上对抗悬锋城之敌。届时我必须说出一段令人印象深刻的出场词，不然就会让伙友的身份蒙羞……
Yes. One day, I will take to the field and battle Castrum Kremnos' enemies. When the day comes, my opening lines must leave the deepest impression, or I will disgrace the vanguards...
嗯…气势有了，但十个是不是有点太少？
Mm... It's got flair, but is ten too few?
听说欧利庞王当年御驾亲征，在战场上杀死了足足一百个敌人！以我悬锋伙友的身份，至少也得打二十个才行。
I heard that when King Eurypon personally fought in battle, he slew a hundred enemies on the field! As a Kremnos vanguard, I have to at least take out twenty.
怎么，没听说过？像你这么青涩的新兵蛋子可不多了……
What, never heard of this before? There aren't many greenhorn recruits like you around...
悬锋伙友是悬锋城最精锐的士兵。我们拥有整座城邦最好的武器和最严整的军纪，直接对欧利庞王负责，其他士兵也以我们为榜样。

The Kremnos vanguard are the most elite soldiers in Castrum Kremnos. We possess the finest weapons and most stringent military discipline in all the city, and are led directly by King Eurypon. Other soldiers hold us in the highest regard.
「王翼冠军」克拉特鲁斯曾赠与我一面盾牌，鼓励我在战场上寻得荣耀…可这么多天过去了，我还是只能在这里站岗，没有机会离开城邦、征讨外敌。

Krateros of the King's Aegis, once gifted me a shield, encouraging me to garner glory on the battlefield... But many days have passed, and yet I remain still at this post — no opportunity to leave the city and quell enemy forces.
虽说保卫王城义不容辞，但对{RUBY_B#悬锋战士}歌耳戈之子{RUBY_E#}们来说，有什么能比战死沙场更显荣耀呢？
We are duty-bound to protect the capital, but as {RUBY_B#Kremnos Warriors}children of Gorgo{RUBY_E#}, what greater glory is there than spilling our lifeblood on the battlefield?
所以我一遍遍练习，等待着用枪尖刺穿敌人、或被敌人用枪尖刺穿的那天。
So I practice every strike, over and over, waiting for the day I will thrust the tip of my lance through an enemy, or be impaled by my enemy.
终有一日，属于我的时刻会到来。我的死会让敌人心有余悸，而战友们会对我的棺材洒下热泪……
One fine day, my moment will arrive. My death will haunt the enemy with lingering fear, and my comrades will weep passionately over my coffin...
这便是我设想的，一个战士最好的结局。
That's the ideal I harbor — the warrior's ideal ending.
宁战死，毋荣归！我的长矛今天也在寻找敌寇。
Valorous death before glorious return! My lance seeks the enemy, even today.
好，言语的交锋亦是战场，我们都能从中学到许多。
Sure, a war of words is also a battlefield. We can learn much from within.
克拉特鲁斯大人是王翼冠军，专职辅佐欧利庞王。作为老兵，他经验丰富，战甲和身上满是刀痕——荣耀的象征。
Lord Krateros is a King's Aegis, whose sole duty is to assist King Eurypon. His experience is unrivaled as a veteran soldier, and both his body and armor bear countless scars — all symbols of glory.
像他这样的精锐总共只有五名。他们是悬锋伙友中的佼佼者，也是王最亲密的战友。当王不在时，他们就是军中位阶最高的将领。

He is one of only five who hold such elite status. They are exemplars among the Kremnos vanguards, and most bosom comrades of the King. When the king is not around, they wield the utmost authority among the ranks.
据说有一次，王亲自率军攻打奥赫玛，一路高歌猛进，却在追击时不慎坠入了奥赫玛人设置的陷坑中……
It's said that when the king personally led the army in a siege against Okhema, their warpath was paved with fury and song, but they carelessly fell into a dastardly Okheman trap...
就在王即将被俘的关键时刻，克拉特鲁斯一手剑、一手矛，硬生生撕开敌军的包围圈，带着王杀了出去。
In that critical moment when the king was about to be captured, Krateros tore through the enemy encirclement with nothing but a sword and shield, the king right behind him.
战后，王亲手赐给他一面银色盾牌，以表彰他临危救主的功绩…要是我也有机会留下这等伟业就好了。
After the battle, the king gifted him with a silver shield, honoring his feat of saving the king... What I would give to have this chance to perform such a heroic deed.
我放在家里了…盾牌可是荣耀的象征，万一留下了划痕，不就有损荣耀了吗？
I left it at home... That shield is a symbol of glory. If I accidentally scratched it, that would be a taint on glory itself!
别笑话我。虽然悬锋城盾的质量远胜其他城邦，但制作方式早已失传，现存的每一面都是上古遗物，经不起折腾。

You might think it a joke, but while the quality of Castrum Kremnos shields far surpasses the smithing capabilities of other cities, their methods of production are long lost. Every shield in existence is an ancient heirloom, and can't withstand much roughhousing.
再说了，一昧格挡可不是战士之道，进攻才是最好的防守。如果每次战斗都带上它，我哪还有机会追求荣耀之死？
Besides, blocking and parrying isn't a warrior's way. Offense is the best defense. If I had to bring it into every battle, where would I get a chance to pursue a glorious death?
不过，听说有些战士掌握飞盾的技巧，而且扔出去还能飞回来…这可比标枪方便多了。或许我也该学一手？
But I hear some warriors are masters at throwing shields, and have their shield return to them after they throw it out... So much more convenient than javelins. Perhaps it's time I learn to do that?
我要怎样成为一名战士？
How can I become a warrior?
战士，最重要的当然是荣耀。悬锋城有句古话：「身可死，荣耀不可弃。」而证明荣耀的最好方式，就是战胜强敌。

Glory is of course the most essential for warriors. Castrum Kremnos has an ancient saying: "The body may die, but glory cannot be abandoned." What better way to prove your honor than to topple a formidable foe?
我听说城外有种名叫{RUBY_B#「执刃黑绒」}钢爪兽{RUBY_E#}的怪物。它擅长掘土，指爪夸张如剑，足以在一挥间斩断精工兵器。一般的士兵根本不是对手。

I hear that there's a type of monster called a {RUBY_B#Bladed Black Velvet}Steelclaw Beast{RUBY_E#} roaming outside the city. It excels at burrowing with claws as long as swords on its paws, capable of cleaving through the finest armaments in a flash. Ordinary soldiers aren't a match for it.
只要能杀死一头那样的怪物，你就算是合格的悬锋战士了，但难点在于如何找到活着的钢爪兽…它们都快被杀光了。
If you can slay such a monster, you'd be more than qualified to be a Kremnos Warrior. Alas, the problem lies in finding a live Steelclaw Beast... They've almost all been killed off.
好在如今还有另一个选择——来自黑潮的怪物。据说它们有千眼百手，浑身尖刺，足以让最勇敢的战士不寒而栗……

Good thing there's another option now — monsters from the black tide. They're said to have hundreds of eyes and arms, their bodies lined with spines, leaving even the bravest of warriors quaking in their boots...
有机会的话，我也想杀两只试试看呐。
I'd also like to try slaying a couple, if I had the chance.
尤利克赛斯？那个懦夫？哼，当然听说过……
Ulixes? That coward? Hmph, of course I've heard of him...
他自诩是新兵的教官，却连半点荣耀都不教，整天说什么「宁失荣耀，勿失性命」——这是战士该有的信条吗？

He fancies himself an instructor of new recruits, but his teachings contain nary a shred of glory. Instead, he harps all day about how "you can forsake glory, but never your life" — is that how a warrior should live his creed?
据说他曾经带队阻击黑潮。他的战友们都选择了光荣赴死，只有他自己活了下来…呵，怕不是当了逃兵！
I hear he once led a company to fight against the black tide. His comrades all chose death in glory, leaving only him alive... Hah, he could have been a deserter for all we know!
我看不惯这种亵渎荣耀的家伙，就当面向他提出决斗，结果被他用阴招放倒了…之后非找他堂堂正正再打一场不可！
I'm ever-disdainful of scum who desecrate glory like him, so I confronted him to a duel, but was bested by his conniving tricks... I will be sure to seek him out for another fight in future!
好，接下来是中场休息时间。
All right, it's time for a break anyway.
多谢了，伙计，我相信会有那一天的。
Thank you, friend. I trust that day will come.
我不是说了吗？前一批斗士的试炼还没结束，你得先耐心等待。
Didn't I already mention it? The trial for the previous batch of gladiators isn't over. You'll have to wait.
而且这是同时考验三人的试炼，你的同伴在哪里？
And this is a trial for groups of three. Where are your companions?
呵！我闭关修炼了二十年，早就忘记怎么交朋友了。你让我去找两个志同道合的伙伴，不是存心刁难我吗？
Hah! I've been training for twenty years as a recluse, and have long forgotten how to make friends. Now you're making me find two like-minded comrades? That's deliberate sabotage!
在战场上，懂得协作的战士远比孤军奋战的莽夫有用。回去吧，你不配在悬锋的光辉下献身。
On the battlefield, warriors who can work together are far more useful than solitary brutes. Go back. You are not worthy of sacrificing yourself under the radiance of Kremnos.
没人能夺走我的荣耀！既然你执意阻止我——那就来碰一碰吧！
None will wrest my glory from me! Since you stand in my way, prove yourself with your blade!
怒火在这位报名官的肌肤下涌动，她就要爆发了。
A burning fire boils beneath the skin of this registration officer. She is about to explode.
不用，我自己能应付——得让他吃点苦头才行。
No need, I will handle it myself. He has to be taught a lesson.
战斗结束得非常迅速——看似瘦弱的报名官仅用一回合就将那斗士摔倒在地。后者连滚带爬地逃走了，狼狈不堪。
The battle is over in an instant — the seemingly feeble officer needs only one stroke to topple that gladiator. The latter stumbles away frantically in retreat, a most miserable sight.
啧，杂碎！说要捍卫荣耀，结果连爬起来再战的勇气都没有。就这点觉悟，还敢挑战尼卡多利的祭司？
Tsk, pathetic! All that hot air about defending glory — he couldn't even muster enough courage to get up and fight again. One with such shallow resolve dares challenge a priest of Nikador?
…你也是来参加试炼的吗？现在不行，我想我应该不用重复一遍。
...Are you here for the trial too? Not now, and I doubt I need to repeat myself.
We've met before. Do you not remember?
参赛者有那么多，我可记不住每一张脸。如果你说见过，那就当是见过吧。
There are many participants. I can't remember every face. If you say we've met, so be it.
I'm not joining. Just looking around.
没有勇气向尼卡多利献出生命吗？倒是可以理解。你面相年轻，不必急着追寻荣耀之死。
Too cowardly to offer your life for Nikador? Understandable. You look young. There's no need to hurtle to your death in search of glory.
你就站在此地，不要走动。
Stay here and don't wander around.
…不明白你在说什么。身为主持祭典的尼卡多利祭司，我当然会留在这里，履行使命。
...I don't understand. As Nikador's priest in charge of festivities, I will of course remain here and fulfill my duty.
{RUBY_B#尼卡多利}庇佑我们的泰坦{RUBY_E#}不爱和平，唯有争斗才能赢得它的睥睨。而为争斗献出生命，是每名勇士必定的终点。
{RUBY_B#Nikador}The Titan that protects us{RUBY_E#} abhors peace. Only war will win their favor. And offering one's life in battle is the inevitable ending for every warrior.
如果在追逐纷争的路上有什么疑虑，可以来找我，我会帮你斩断它们。
Should doubt arise on your path to pursuing strife, you may seek me out, and I will help you sever it.
只有英雄的灵魂才能立于尼卡多利左右。战士们，献上你们的身躯、努力去成为英雄吧！
Only a heroic soul may stand by Nikador's side. Warriors, give your bodies, and strive to become heroes!
疑虑是战士的大敌。说出来，我会帮你解答它。
Doubt is the nemesis of all warriors. I will help you break it.
尼卡多利的祭司是什么样？
What is a priest of Nikador like?
尼卡多利可不像那些懦弱的神祇。它的祭品不是鲜花、泥土和美食，而是纷争中流下的鲜血。
Nikador is unlike those cowardly deities. It demands tribute not in the form of flowers, soil, or food, but fresh blood flowing from strife.
但纷争并不总意味着死亡，单纯的斗殴也能带来鲜血。尼卡多利的祭司们个个都是摔跤好手，通过肉体的碰撞来敬神。
But strife does not always mean death. Even a plain old brawl draws blood. All of Nikador's priests are master grapplers, worshiping our god with the clashing of our flesh.
要知道，军队在出征之前总会向尼卡多利祈求赐福。那可不是什么沐浴焚香、念诵祷文的仪式，而是一场大混战……
You must know that armies pray for Nikador's blessing before they deploy. It is not a ceremony of baths in fragrant oils and incense, nor is it solemn prayers from texts, but a melee of chaos...
足足五十名祭司在阵前角力、拳斗，招招见血；士兵们高举手中的鲜血蜜酿，一有兴趣便亲自下场，失手打死人也是常有的事。

Fifty priests, lined up in formation, wrestle and punch, each strike drawing blood. Soldiers raise their hands high, cupped with honey brew mixed in blood. Those interested will take to the ring as well. Accidental deaths in fights are commonplace.
我已经活过了整整三轮混战…之前那斗士竟然还敢挑战我，真是不自量力。
I have survived three full cycles of this melee... For that gladiator to even dare challenge me is utter idiocy.
What do you think of the festival?
我是尼卡多利的祭司，自然支持王的决议。我也参加过其他泰坦的祭典，根本没有这么热闹，像是小姑娘刺绣。

I am a priest of Nikador, and naturally uphold our king's decision. I have also partaken in festivals of other Titans. They are never as bustling as ours — more akin to a woman's embroidery than a festival.
这倒不奇怪。奥赫玛人从来都不擅长对抗性运动，只热衷于雕刻、舞蹈和诗词——悬锋人在这些方面也不差。
It is not surprising. Okhemans have never excelled at competitive sports, only immersing themselves in sculpting, dance and poetry, though Kremnoans aren't too shabby at these either.
雅努斯的信徒还喜欢一种名叫「轮回棋」的游戏，用扔木棒来走棋。他们说下棋能精进自我、陶冶情操…无聊透顶。
Janus' disciples prefer a game called Cyclicality Chess, throwing wooden sticks to move chess pieces. They say that chess can elevate oneself and cultivate one's character... How banal.
悬锋人提倡的是争抢球、摔跤和越障跑。这些运动既能磨炼我们的技艺，也能唤醒我们体内的热血，让我们变得更快、更强。

Kremnoans advocate games where people fight over balls, wrestling, and obstacle races. These sports can hone our technique and summon the blood in our bodies to the fore, allowing us to become faster and stronger.
毕竟，唯有英雄的灵魂才能立于尼卡多利左右，弱者与懦夫只会被塞纳托斯拾走。
After all, only a heroic soul can stand alongside Nikador. The weak and cowardly will only be taken away by Thanatos.
Let us speak again when chance allows.
做好准备，尼卡多利的召唤随时都可能降临。
Get ready. Nikador's summoning may descend at any time.
摒弃疑虑，才能更好地为尼卡多利献身。
Only by relinquishing all doubt can we best devote ourselves to Nikador.
哗啦啦…哗啦啦……浪潮的声音再次于你耳边响起……
*splssh* ... *splssh* ...The sounds of waves reach your ears once again...
你听到波浪缓缓翻涌、汹涌的狂涛、仿佛这小小盆中藏着一片无边的大海。而那海声中还夹杂着奇异的低语——
You hear the gentle rolling of waves, their tumultuous surges, like an endless expanse of ocean contained in this small bowl. And strange whispers mingle amidst tidal roars...
见了鬼了，水怎么会渴——不对，水怎么会说话！？
I'm hearing ghosts. How can water be thirsty... Wait, how can water be talking!?
宝贝儿…仙馔秘酿…灌进来…我给你馈赠……
My dear... Celestial Ambrosia... Pour some in... I'll give you a present...
Ignore it. Listen to the Voice of Constellation
What gift is that? Lemme see.
宝贝儿…带秘酿来…珍宝、圣物、美人儿…随你挑……
My dear... Bring ambrosia here... Treasures, relics, beauties... Take your pick...
水面恢复了平静…真是稀奇，看来你以后只要带着仙馔秘酿来，就可以在这儿讨点好处。
The water returns to its serene state... How curious — it appears that you just have to bring Celestial Ambrosia back here next time to get something nice.
小宝贝儿…带秘酿来了吗？快点~
My dear... Did you bring me ambrosia? Hurry...
Listen to the Voice of Constellation.
Chat... I can't... without something to drink...
对了，我是个醒不来的醉客…不，我是个风流成性的舞者…不不不，我是个耽迷纵欲的君王……
Oh yes, I am a drunk that can't sober up... No, I am a dancer frolicking among my loves... No, I am a king indulgent in desires of flesh...
不不不不…我是水里的鱼，很大的鱼…很大很大…有很多秘酿，好喝的秘酿被鱼喝光了……别喝了…我的…是我的，我要……
Nononono... I am a fish in water, a big fish... Large, huge... So much ambrosia, tasty ambrosia all drank by the fish... Don't drink anymore... Mine... It's all mine, I want it...
我要…要去大鱼嘴里的宴会…在…大海！对，在大海！大海装在醉客的杯子里，宴会上的暴君被珊瑚灌醉了，舞女在和礁石拥吻！

I want... I want to go to the banquet in the giant fish's mouth... in... the sea! Yes, the sea! The sea in the drunkard's glass, the tyrant at the banquet inebriated by corals, the dancers embraced in locked kisses with rocky reefs!
嘻嘻…哈哈…哈哈哈哈哈…我要秘酿…把酒杯斟得更满些吧……
Heehee... Haha... Hahahaha... I want ambrosia... Fill up the wine glass to its brim...
秘酿…仙馔秘酿是至宝！用大地的身体发酵…灌进天空的云朵里…他生气了，嘻嘻嘻……
Ambrosia... Celestial Ambrosia is supreme! Fermented with the land's body... encased in the sky's clouds... He's angry, heeheehee...
等等…等等…那不是你们的！是我的，都是我的！
Wait... Wait... That does not belong to you! It's mine, all mine!
还给我…还给我…明明是我和你们共享的…不懂得回报的、自私的凡人…真是……
Give it back... to me... I was the one who shared it with you... Selfish mortals who are oblivious to reciprocation... are really...
真是…嘻、嘻嘻嘻…真是可爱呀…我爱你们…一起喝吧……
Really... Hee, heeheehee... Really adorable... I love you all... Let's all drink up...
没意思的地方…没有宴会、没有音乐、没有秘酿…黑漆漆的………好小，好窄…让我真难受……
A pointless place... No banquets, no music, no ambrosia... Pitch black... So small, so narrow... So uncomfortable...
但我告诉你，宝贝儿…过去这儿可不是这样的…鱼儿们…起舞…醉客们…纵欲、起舞、纵欲……
But let me tell you, my dear... It wasn't like this in the past... The fishes... they dance... The drunks... frolicking, dancing, frolicking...
阳光、酣歌、秘酿…秘酿…我要秘酿……
Merry, rowdy, ambrosia... Ambrosia... I want ambrosia...
宝贝儿…带秘酿来…不管聊天还是别的…我都陪你……
My dear... Bring ambrosia... Chat or otherwise... I'll accompany you...
下次…记得带秘酿来…多带点……
Next time... remember to bring ambrosia... More...
盆中的水面开始泛起涟漪…翻涌不息…潺潺的水声诉说着喜乐，荡漾的波纹像是小小的浪花，仿佛这方寸也成了汪洋——

Ripples start to form in the bowl... rolling endlessly... The murmuring water whispers of revelry, the undulating tidal patterns like tiny waves, like this small space has also become a roiling ocean...
<size=36>Ahh absolute bliss! 'Tis the pinnacle of bliss!</size>
久违的满足，谢谢你，宝贝儿~这些秘酿让我稍微清醒了点…原来你映在水面上的容貌这么俊美，让我都耐不住翻涌起来了呢~
Such long-awaited satisfaction, thank you, my dear! This ambrosia has sobered me up a little... The visage of your reflection is so good-looking that I can't help but get all stirred up...
I think I'm very pretty too.
岁月…过去多久了呢？欧洛尼斯，你动的还真快啊……
Time... How long has passed? Oronyx, you move fast...
这星空…原来如此，她还没放弃…你也是这宴上的愚人呀……
This starry sky... I see, she still hasn't given up... You are also a fool in this banquet...
那就努力起舞…努力纵欲…努力浪费岁月吧…像游向明天的小鱼儿……
Dance hard then... indulge hard... waste time hard... Little fishy that wants to swim to tomorrow...
当你们的宴会落幕…清醒后所见的荒唐，便是买醉的代价……
When your banquet ends... the absurdity that you will see when you sober up will be the price of buying inebriation...
你醒着说话比醉了还难懂。
You speak less sense sober than when you're drunk.
潮汐声音重归涣散，语句如水花般破碎——看来这盆水欲壑难填，是位喂不饱的饮客。
